input.headline: Jobs report blows past expectations, defying hiring slowdown

raw.headline: Jobs report blows past expectations, defying hiring slowdown

winner: Sure, we expected a slowdown in hiring, but no one thought the unemployment line would be *moving backward*.

candidates.text: Why did the job market throw a party? Because the economic data was so hot, it set the hiring pace on fire!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative metaphor with surprising twist, concise and specific to the headline topic"}

candidates.text: Why did the jobs report break up with the hiring slowdown? It said, "We're just not compatible anymore—you keep expecting us to slow down, and I keep defying expectations!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative personification with a clever twist on 'defying expectations,' concise and specific to the headline."}

candidates.text: The economy was so hot, unemployment reports started arriving via express mail—with a note saying "Do Not Open Until Tomorrow.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific metaphor with clever wordplay on 'hot economy' and 'express mail'; concise and natural."}

candidates.text: Sure, we expected a slowdown in hiring, but no one thought the unemployment line would be *moving backward*.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on 'moving backward,' specific to the context of the jobs report, concise, and naturally funny."}

candidates.text: The unemployment rate fell so fast, economists had to check if the labor force just forgot how to exist.
